scope.summary: TTC message type 129 is treated as a transient protocol desynchronization: the session is discarded, the pool performs its one fresh-connection retry, and the agent sees a typed retryable envelope without TTC wire text.
out_of_scope: Changing the pinned driver or protocol decoder.
Claiming a live Oracle/R1 fault-injection run.

entry_points.name: driver query-error seam
entry_points.trace: A synthetic oracledb::Error::Protocol(ProtocolError::UnknownMessageType { message_type: 129, position: 35 }) is promoted to DbError::ConnectionLost, which activates the pool's fresh-connection retry path and DbError::into_envelope emits ErrorClass::Transient with suppressed driver detail.

limitations: Pinned-driver source citation: rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb-protocol/src/dpl.rs:196 and :350 raise ProtocolError::UnknownMessageType; rust-oracledb/crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs:1636-1648 currently treats that protocol variant as reusable/not connection-lost, so the server-side override is deliberate.
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.../target/b13b-check cargo test -p oraclemcp-db ttc_129_is_promoted_to_fresh_connection_retry -- --nocapture passed (1 test).
No live Oracle or external fault-injection evidence was run.
